house_prices.dataset_description: Ames Iowa Housing dataset. Regression task predicting SalePrice from 79 features. 43 categorical columns (many ordinal quality ratings). 6.7% avg missing rate. Id is a row identifier (leakage — already removed). Many quality columns follow Po < Fa < TA < Gd < Ex ordinal scale.

column_semantics.MSZoning: nominal — zoning classification (RL, RM, C, FV, RH)
column_semantics.LotShape: ordinal — lot shape (Reg > IR1 > IR2 > IR3)
column_semantics.Neighborhood: nominal — physical location, high cardinality (25 unique)
column_semantics.ExterQual: ordinal — exterior quality (Po < Fa < TA < Gd < Ex)
column_semantics.ExterCond: ordinal — exterior condition (Po < Fa < TA < Gd < Ex)
column_semantics.BsmtQual: ordinal — basement height quality (Po < Fa < TA < Gd < Ex)
column_semantics.BsmtCond: ordinal — basement condition (Po < Fa < TA < Gd < Ex)
column_semantics.BsmtExposure: ordinal — basement wall exposure (No < Mn < Av < Gd)
column_semantics.BsmtFinType1: ordinal — basement finish type (Unf < LwQ < Rec < BLQ < ALQ < GLQ)
column_semantics.HeatingQC: ordinal — heating quality (Po < Fa < TA < Gd < Ex)
column_semantics.Electrical: ordinal — electrical system (Mix < FuseP < FuseF < FuseA < SBrkr)
column_semantics.KitchenQual: ordinal — kitchen quality (Po < Fa < TA < Gd < Ex)
column_semantics.Functional: ordinal — home functionality (Sal < Sev < Maj2 < Maj1 < Mod < Min2 < Min1 < Typ)
column_semantics.FireplaceQu: ordinal — fireplace quality (Po < Fa < TA < Gd < Ex)
column_semantics.GarageQual: ordinal — garage quality (Po < Fa < TA < Gd < Ex)
column_semantics.GarageCond: ordinal — garage condition (Po < Fa < TA < Gd < Ex)
column_semantics.PoolQC: ordinal — pool quality (Fa < TA < Gd < Ex, mostly missing)
column_semantics.Fence: ordinal — fence quality (MnWw < GdWo < MnPrv < GdPrv)
column_semantics.MiscFeature: nominal — miscellaneous feature (Gar2, Othr, Shed, TenC)

must_do: encode_categorical_per_column with ordinal as default for tree-based efficiency
house_prices.notes: Tree-based model (gbm): ordinal encoding is efficient for all categoricals. Trees split on thresholds, so ordinal values work regardless of natural ordering. Outlier clipping is optional (trees are robust to outliers). Feature selection can reduce overfitting on wide datasets.
